Title: A homeobox transcription factor HB34 suppresses jasmonic acid biosynthesis but promotes the expression of growth-related genes to balance plant immunity and growth in Arabidopsis

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Growth-related gene; downregulated in hb34-1 and induced by HB34
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Reduced in hb34-1; elevated in p35S::HB34-GFP hb34-1 and HB34-ER after estradiol
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes plant growth and development including hypocotyl elongation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression of YUC8 in hb34-1 fully rescues reduced hypocotyl length, leaf area, and fresh weight without suppressing enhanced disease resistance
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Normal growth conditions; Botrytis cinerea infection context; estradiol induction
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. YUC8 is directly bound and activated by HB34. 2. YUC8 expression is reduced in hb34-1 and increased by HB34 overexpression/induction. 3. YUC8 overexpression rescues hb34 growth defects while retaining resistance, uncoupling growth from defense.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; ChIP-seq; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; transient dual-luciferase assay; estradiol-inducible HB34 expression; transgenic overexpression phenotyping
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising engineering target to restore growth without sacrificing immunity in backgrounds with elevated defense
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential auxin overproduction effects not detailed in this paper
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included by authors as a representative growth-related direct target of HB34

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Small Auxin Up RNA family protein
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Growth-related gene; downregulated in hb34-1 and induced by HB34
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Reduced in hb34-1; elevated in p35S::HB34-GFP hb34-1 and HB34-ER after estradiol
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression of SAUR65 in hb34-1 fully rescues growth defects but does not reduce enhanced Botrytis resistance, thereby uncoupling growth and defense
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Normal growth conditions; Botrytis cinerea infection context; estradiol induction
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SAUR65 is directly bound and transcriptionally activated by HB34. 2. SAUR65 expression is reduced in hb34-1 and restored by HB34. 3. SAUR65 overexpression rescues hb34 growth defects without compromising elevated immunity.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; ChIP-seq; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; transient dual-luciferase assay; estradiol-inducible HB34 expression; transgenic overexpression phenotyping
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful downstream target to improve growth in defense-activated plants without weakening resistance
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Representative growth-related downstream gene used to demonstrate decoupling of growth and immunity

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling and growth inhibition responses
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: coi1 mutation suppresses enhanced resistance, constitutive JA-responsive gene activation, JA sensitivity, and growth defects of hb34-1, indicating COI1-dependent JA signaling is required downstream of HB34
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Botrytis cinerea infection; methyl jasmonate treatment
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. COI1 is required for the hb34 mutant phenotypes. 2. hb34-1 coi1 double mutants resemble coi1 in disease susceptibility, JA insensitivity, and growth traits. 3. HB34 therefore acts genetically upstream of COI1-mediated JA signaling.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic crossing/double-mutant analysis; pathogen assay; RT-qPCR; JA sensitivity assays
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Demonstrates that manipulating JA perception can override HB34 effects, though broad pleiotropy may limit application
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced JA signaling compromises resistance to necrotrophic pathogen despite promoting growth
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as a pathway-genetic validator rather than a newly discovered focal mechanistic target

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in hb34-1 seedlings under normal conditions; induced by jasmonate pathway activation and Botrytis cinerea infection context
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes unstable allene oxide to form OPDA in the JA biosynthetic pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms pathway intermediate leading to jasmonic acid
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense against necrotrophic pathogens; mediates growth–defense trade-off and stress responses
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes jasmonic acid biosynthesis; de-repressed in hb34-1 and suppressed by HB34
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Botrytis cinerea infection; methyl jasmonate treatment; normal growth conditions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AOC1 is a direct HB34-bound jasmonate biosynthetic gene. 2. AOC1 transcript abundance increases in hb34-1 and decreases upon HB34 induction/overexpression. 3. Its elevated expression is associated with increased JA accumulation and defense activation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; ChIP-seq; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; transient dual-luciferase assay; estradiol-inducible HB34 expression
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for tuning JA accumulation and defense-growth balance, but repression may reduce immunity while promoting growth
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Increased AOC1/JA is linked to growth inhibition; reduced AOC1/JA may compromise resistance
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Selected by authors as one of three representative direct JA-biosynthetic targets of HB34

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in hb34-1 seedlings under normal conditions; JA-responsive
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxygenates α-linolenic acid to produce 13-HPOT, an early precursor in JA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for jasmonic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense against necrotrophic pathogens; mediates growth inhibition and stress signaling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: HB34 represses LOX2 transcription; increased LOX2 expression in hb34-1 contributes to elevated JA pathway activity and enhanced defense with growth penalty
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key upstream entry step in pathway, but rate-limiting status not established in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Botrytis cinerea infection; methyl jasmonate treatment; normal growth conditions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LOX2 is directly bound by HB34 in vivo and in vitro. 2. HB34 represses LOX2 promoter activity and transcript accumulation. 3. Elevated LOX2 expression in hb34-1 correlates with constitutive JA pathway activation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; ChIP-seq; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; transient dual-luciferase assay; estradiol-inducible HB34 expression
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to modulate JA precursor formation for balancing disease resistance and growth
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Activation favors defense but inhibits growth; repression may reduce JA-mediated resistance
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Chosen as a representative JA-biosynthetic gene directly controlled by HB34

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Old yellow enzyme-like oxophytodienoate reductase family
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in hb34-1 seedlings under normal conditions; JA pathway-associated
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: OPC-8:0 precursor downstream of OPDA reduction en route to jasmonic acid
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces OPDA in the JA biosynthetic pathway leading toward jasmonic acid formation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate downstream intermediate required for jasmonic acid production
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling against necrotrophs; regulates growth-defense trade-off
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: HB34 directly represses OPR3; genetic disruption of OPR3 suppresses enhanced resistance and growth defects of hb34-1 by blocking JA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step supported genetically because opr3 mutation suppresses hb34 phenotypes
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Botrytis cinerea infection; methyl jasmonate treatment; normal growth conditions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OPR3 is a direct transcriptional target repressed by HB34. 2. opr3 mutation suppresses both elevated resistance and growth defects of hb34-1, placing OPR3 downstream of HB34. 3. OPR3-dependent JA biosynthesis is required for hb34 phenotypes.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; ChIP-seq; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; transient dual-luciferase assay; estradiol-inducible HB34 expression; genetic crossing/double-mutant analysis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for engineering defense-growth balance by reducing JA biosynthesis downstream of HB34
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of OPR3 reduces JA-dependent defense and JA sensitivity while enhancing growth
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Unlike AOC1 and LOX2, OPR3 was also genetically tested through the hb34-1 opr3 double mutant

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Gradually increases from seedling to adult stage; reduced by methyl jasmonate treatment and Botrytis cinerea infection; elevated in opr3, coi1, and myc2 mutants
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Same as source species because study is in Arabidopsis Col-0; expressed under normal conditions and suppressed by JA pathway activation/infection
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense against necrotrophic pathogens and mediator of growth-defense trade-off
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Jasmonic acid biosynthesis and growth-related transcriptional program
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses AOC1, LOX2, and OPR3; activates YUC8 and SAUR65
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key regulator that prevents excessive JA accumulation under normal conditions while promoting growth, thereby balancing immunity and growth
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of HB34 increases JA accumulation, JA-responsive gene expression, anthocyanin accumulation, and Botrytis cinerea resistance but impairs growth; overexpression causes opposite trends
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Jasmonate pathway; Botrytis cinerea infection; MeJA treatment; likely negative input from JA signaling including COI1/MYC2 pathway
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression of JA biosynthetic genes and transcriptional activation of growth-related genes
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Negative feedback with JA pathway: JA treatment/infection lowers HB34 expression, while JA-defective/signaling mutants elevate HB34 transcript
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Botrytis cinerea infection; methyl jasmonate treatment; estradiol induction in HB34-ER lines
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HB34 is the central regulator identified in this study that balances growth and immunity in Arabidopsis. 2. HB34 directly represses JA biosynthetic genes AOC1, LOX2, and OPR3 to limit JA accumulation. 3. HB34 directly activates growth-related genes such as YUC8 and SAUR65, and overexpression of these targets rescues hb34 growth defects without reducing resistance.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA mutant analysis; CRISPR knockout; overexpression complementation; RNA-seq; ChIP-seq; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; transient dual-luciferase assay; inducible expression; JA quantification; pathogen assays; double-mutant analysis
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss improves resistance but causes strong growth penalty; overexpression promotes growth but reduces resistance
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High value regulator for decoupling growth-defense trade-off; could be combined with growth-gene overexpression or targeted promoter editing to fine-tune JA suppression without excessive susceptibility
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: HB23 is a close homolog with weaker similar phenotype, but authors focused subsequent mechanistic work on HB34
